Export.zv: Cache for UNIX (Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86-64) 2014.1.3 (Build 775)
Export.Routine: DVPUTL ;DAL/BNT - Digial Veterans Platform Utility Functions;5/19/2017 ;;1.0;DIGITAL VETERANS PLATFORM;****;October 1, 2015;Build 58 ; ;Per VA Directive 6402, this routine should not be modified. ;This routine uses a SACC Exemption to section 2.4.5.2 (use of exclusive KILL) ; The INIT routine label contains the only use of exclusive kill. ; ; ; External References DBIA# ; ------------------- ----- ; DOLRO^%ZOSV 3883 ; FIELD^DID 2052 ; FILE^DID 2052 ; $$VFIELD^DILFD 2055 ; $$VFILE^DILFD 2055 Q ; ;--------- Supported Methods for external application use with DVP ---------- ; INIT(SOURCE,TARGET) ; Initialize Common DVP Variables ;Clear symbol table of all but values from this call ; The following call is subject to a SACC exemption for section 2.4.5.2 ; This call is necessary to ensure that later symbol table restores ; are not inhibited by existing local variables ; This call also ensures that an existing session is not incorrectly used to make a call. ;K (DUZ,DVPERR,RESULTS,DVPSYM,SOURCE,TARGET) ;S $ET="S E=$$ERR^DVPERR($NA(^TMP(""DVP_TARGET"",$J)))" ; All Public functions return DVPERR. ; A value of 1 = Successful call with results in the TARGET array ; A value of 0 = Error occurred somewhere with details in the ERROR global S DVPERR=1 ; This should only be necessary if session state is not maintained I $G(U)="" S U="^" D NOW^%DTC S DT=X I TARGET'=SOURCE K @TARGET I $G(@SOURCE@("DVPSYM"))=1 S DVPSYM=1 D . N CNT,D,I,T S I=$G(SOURCE) Q:I="" S T=$E(I,1,$L(I)-1) . F S I=$Q(@I) Q:I=""!(I'[T) D . . S NODE=$P(I,T,2),NODE=$P(NODE,",",2,999),NODE=$TR(NODE,")",""),NODE=$TR(NODE,"""","") . . Q:$P(NODE,",",1)["input" . . S @TARGET@("input",NODE)=@I S RESULTS=$NA(^TMP($J,"DVP","TMP","RESULTS")) K @RESULTS Q ; INITPARM(PARAM) ; Initialize Input Parameters to empty string if null N X,DVPTYPE,DVPNAME S PARAM=$$UP^XLFSTR($E(PARAM,1,8)) S (X,DVPTYPE)="" F S X=$O(@SOURCE@("param",X)) Q:X="" D . S DVPTYPE=$P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U,2) . I DVPTYPE="literal" D Q . . S DVPNAME=$E($$UP^XLFSTR($P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U)),1,8) . . I PARAM=DVPNAME S @PARAM=$P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U,3) I ($G(@PARAM)="null")!($G(@PARAM)="") S @PARAM="" Q ; RESULT() ; This is the primary function to call to complete a DVP service call N STATEGBL D CLEANUP I +$G(DVPSYM) D SAVESYM^DVPUTL($TR(TARGET,")",",")_"""symbolTable"""_",") Q DVPERR ; CONVERT(IN) ; Convert input to camelCase N I,UP,LOW,OTHIN,OTHOUT,OUT,X S UP="ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ" S LOW="abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" S OTHIN="/-&()*.?" S OTHOUT="________" S OUT="" F I=1:1:$L(IN," ") S X=$P(IN," ",I) D . I I=1 S OUT=OUT_$TR(X,UP,LOW) Q . S OUT=OUT_$TR($E(X,1),"*","_")_$TR($E(X,2,$L(X)),UP_OTHIN,LOW_OTHOUT) Q OUT ; GETFILE(FILE) ; Return the File Name from the file number N RSLT S RSLT="" D FILE^DID(FILE,"","NAME","RSLT") Q RSLT("NAME") ; GETFLD(FILE,FIELD) ; Return the Field Name for a file N RSLT S RSLT="" D FIELD^DID(FILE,FIELD,"","LABEL","RSLT") Q $G(RSLT("LABEL")) ; BLDERROR(CODE,TEXT,ACTOR) ; Build a generic Error code and text into the TARGET array S @RESULTS@("DIERR",1)=CODE S @RESULTS@("DIERR",1,"TEXT",1)=TEXT D ERROR(RESULTS,ACTOR) Q ; ;--------- Private Methods used only by Digial Veterans Platform ---------- ; GETS(RESULTS,TARGET) ; ; Parse RESULTS of GETS^DIQ and convert to camelCase output in the TARGET array N CNT,FILE,PFILE,REVIENS,FLDNAME,IENS,FIELD,WPFLD,FNAME,RSLT S CNT=0 S FILE="" F S FILE=$O(@RESULTS@(FILE)) Q:FILE="" D . ; Increment File/Sub-File counter . S CNT=CNT+1 . ; Get the Primary File name . I CNT=1 S FNAME=$$CONVERT($$GETFILE(FILE)) D BLDFILE(FILE,TARGET) . ; Get the Sub-File name(s) from the .01 field of the Sub-File and indicate the Primary file processing is complete . I CNT>1 S FNAME=$$CONVERT($$GETFLD(FILE,.01)) . Q:FNAME="" . S IENS="" F S IENS=$O(@RESULTS@(FILE,IENS)) Q:IENS="" D . . ; Reverse the order of the IENS . . S REVIENS=$$REVERSE(IENS) . . ; Get the Field Name . . S FIELD="" F S FIELD=$O(@RESULTS@(FILE,IENS,FIELD)) Q:FIELD="" D . . . ; Convert field name to camelCase . . . S FLDNAME=$$CONVERT(FIELD) . . . S WPFLD="" F S WPFLD=$O(@RESULTS@(FILE,IENS,FIELD,WPFLD)) Q:WPFLD="" D . . . . ; Don't include the Word Processing Internal/External values from the RESULTS array . . . . Q:@RESULTS@(FILE,IENS,FIELD,WPFLD)[$P(RESULTS,",") . . . . I WPFLD'="E" S @TARGET@("results",REVIENS,FNAME,FLDNAME,$S(WPFLD="I":"internalValue",1:WPFLD))=@RESULTS@(FILE,IENS,FIELD,WPFLD) Q . . . . S @TARGET@("results",REVIENS,FNAME,FLDNAME)=@RESULTS@(FILE,IENS,FIELD,WPFLD) Q ; LIST(FILE,RESULTS,TARGET) ; ; Parse RESULTS of LIST^DIC and convert to camelCase output in the TARGET array N ID,IEN,NODE,VALUE,MAP,RSLT,FNAME,HDR,I,ELEMENT S FNAME=$$CONVERT($$GETFILE(FILE)) ; Set the File info in Target array D BLDFILE(FILE,TARGET) ; Get the Header Node data element F I=1:1:$L(@RESULTS@("DILIST",0),U) S HDR=$P(@RESULTS@("DILIST",0),U,I) Q:HDR="" D . S @TARGET@("results","header",$S(I=1:"numberFound",I=2:"maxRequested",I=3:"moreFound",1:"resultsFlags"))=$S(I=3:$S(+HDR:"true",1:"false"),1:HDR) ; Get the MAP for the data elements S MAP=@RESULTS@("DILIST",0,"MAP") S ID=0 F S ID=$O(@RESULTS@("DILIST",ID)) Q:'ID D . N I F I=1:1:$L(@RESULTS@("DILIST",ID,0),U) S VALUE=$P(@RESULTS@("DILIST",ID,0),U,I) Q:VALUE="" D . . ; Set the subscript node to either the ID or the name of the field . . S NODE=$S(I=1:"id",1:$$CONVERT($$GETFLD(FILE,$P(MAP,U,I)))) . . Q:NODE="" . . S @TARGET@("results",FNAME,ID,NODE)=VALUE Q $$RESULT() ; BLDFILE(FILE,TARGET) ; Build the top level File data in the TARGET array S @TARGET@("results","fileNumber")=FILE S @TARGET@("results","fileName")=$$GETFILE(FILE) Q ; REVERSE(IN) ; Reverse the IENS so that File IEN is before any Sub-File IEN N I,OUT,C S C="," S OUT="" F I=($L(IN,C)-1):-1:1 S OUT=OUT_$P(IN,C,I)_C Q OUT ; ERROR(ERRRES,ACTOR) ; Return a list of Error Codes from GETS^DIQ and return the Target array D ERROR^DVPERR(@ERRRES@("DIERR",1),ACTOR,"",@ERRRES@("DIERR",1,"TEXT",1)) Q ; ROUTINE(ENTRY,ROUTINE,PARAMS,RES) ; Build a Method signature with parameters and execute it N METHSIG,MSIG S MSIG=$$MTHDSIG(ROUTINE,ENTRY) I MSIG="" D BLDERROR(1,"Method Signature "_ENTRY_"^"_ROUTINE_" does not exist.","ROUTINE^DVPUTL") Q I $G(PARAMS)]"" S METHSIG=ENTRY_"^"_ROUTINE_"(.RES,"_$G(PARAMS)_")" D @METHSIG Q ; Need to handle the case for no input params S METHSIG=ENTRY_"^"_ROUTINE_"(.RES)" D @METHSIG Q ; CLEANUP ; Destroy Common Variables on Exit I $G(RESULTS)]"" K @RESULTS Q ; VFILE(FILE,TARGET) ; Verify a file ; Input: FILE = File number ; TARGET = Target array to return error data ; Output: 0 if valid ; I FILE="" D BLDERROR(1,"Missing FILE parameter","VFILE^DVPUTL") Q DVPERR I '$$VFILE^DILFD(FILE) D BLDERROR(1,"File "_FILE_" does not exist","VFILE^DVPUTL") Q DVPERR ; VFIELD(FILE,FIELD,CODES,TARGET) ; Verify that FIELD is valid ; Input: FILE = File number ; FIELD = Field List ; CODES = List of allowable Field Codes, if any ; TARGET = Target array to return error data ; Output: 0 if valid N FLD,ERR,I S FLD="",ERR=0 F I=1:1:$L(FIELD,";") S FLD=$P(FIELD,";",I) Q:FLD="" D Q:+ERR . ; If this field is in the list of allowable codes don't throw error . I FLD[CODES Q . ; Verify beginning and ending range of fields . I FLD[":" N FLDRNG,X,J S FLDRNG=FLD,X="" F J=1:1:$L(FLDRNG,":") S X=$P(FLDRNG,":",J) Q:(X="")!(+ERR) D Q . . I '$$VFIELD^DILFD(FILE,X) S ERR=1 . I '$$VFIELD^DILFD(FILE,FLD) S ERR=1 I ERR D BLDERROR(2,"Field "_FLD_" is not a valid field in File "_FILE_".","VFIELD^DVPUTL") Q DVPERR ; VFLAGS(FLAGS,INPUT,TARGET) ; Verify that the Flags are valid ; Input: FLAGS = Allowable flags ; INPUT = Flags passed into API ; TARGET = Target array to return error data ; Output: 0 if valid N I,X F I=1:1:$L(INPUT) S X=$E(INPUT,I) I FLAGS'[X D Q . D BLDERROR(3,"The Flag "_X_" does not match allowable Flags "_FLAGS_".","VFLAGS^DVPUTL") Q DVPERR ; GETPARMS(SOURCE,PARAMS) ; Get the parameters N X,TYPE,ARRNAME,VALUE,NAME K PARAMS S (X,TYPE)="" F S X=$O(@SOURCE@("param",X)) Q:X="" D . S TYPE=$P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U,2) . I TYPE="literal" D Q . . S NAME=$P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U) . . S @NAME=$P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U,3) . . S $P(PARAMS,",",X)=NAME . ; Must be a List Array type . I TYPE="list" D . . ; Build the input array with values. . . S ARRNAME=$E($$UP^XLFSTR($P(@SOURCE@("param",X),U)),1,8) . . S INDEX="" F S INDEX=$O(@SOURCE@("param",X,INDEX)) Q:INDEX="" D . . . S VALUE=@SOURCE@("param",X,INDEX) . . . S @ARRNAME@(INDEX)=VALUE . . . ; Get Local Array data . . . S $P(PARAMS,",",X)="."_ARRNAME S DUZ=$G(@SOURCE@("DUZ")) Q ; MTHDSIG(RTN,TAG) ; Load a routine and return the Method Signature N X,MSIG,DIF,XCNP S MSIG="" S X=RTN,XCNP=0,DIF="^UTILITY("_$J_",RTN," K ^UTILITY($J,RTN) X ^%ZOSF("TEST") Q:'$T MSIG X ^%ZOSF("LOAD") S ^UTILITY($J,RTN,0)=XCNP-1 I '$D(^UTILITY($J,RTN,0)) Q MSIG N X S X=1 F S X=$O(^UTILITY($J,RTN,X)) Q:X="" D . Q:$E(^UTILITY($J,RTN,X,0),1)="" . Q:$E(^UTILITY($J,RTN,X,0),1,$L(TAG))'=TAG . ; If TAG matches, make sure the next char is '(' to allow for matching entry with additional text . Q:$E(^UTILITY($J,RTN,X,0),$L(TAG)+1)'="(" . S MSIG=^UTILITY($J,RTN,X,0) Q MSIG ; SAVESYM(X) ; Save the Symbol Table to global X D DOLRO^%ZOSV M @TARGET@("input")=SOURCE Q ; AUTH() ;Function. Check if calling routine is authorized ; This function does not address the security issue of user authentication, so a restriction is placed on the calling routine. ; ZEXCEPT: XTMUNIT,XTU ;set for unit testing N X,Z S X=$ST($ST-2,"PLACE"),Z=$P(X,"^",2),X="^"_$P(Z," ") I $E(X,1,4)="^DVP" Q 1 ;Authorized Kernel DVP access I $D(XTMUNIT)!$G(XTU) Q 1 ;Kernel Unit Testing Q 0
